criteria.criterion: RecoverFromWAL no longer silently no-ops on btree WAL records in a way that loses data
criteria.covered_by: TestCrashBetweenInsertAndSaveRootRecovers (proves the recovery path that matters in practice -- root-pointer recovery via LoadRoot -- no longer silently drops inserts across a crash; catalog.RecoverFromWAL's default no-op on RecordBTreeInsert/RecordBTreeDelete remains intentional and unchanged since no production code writes those record types)

criteria.criterion: SaveRoot invoked automatically (not only manually) so a crash between an Insert and the next SaveRoot no longer drops that insert
criteria.covered_by: engine/btree/insert.go: Tree.Insert (bootstrap) + propagate (root split) both now call SaveRoot automatically

criteria.criterion: Test spec: go test ./engine/btree/... -race -run TestCrashBetweenInsertAndSaveRootRecovers -- insert, crash before next SaveRoot, recover, assert insert present
No regression in ordinary (non-root-changing) insert/delete/lookup behavior or performance-sensitive concurrent tests
criteria.covered_by: full -race suite for engine/btree (includes TestConcurrentMixedWorkload, TestConcurrentMixedWorkloadForcedLookupDuringDelete, TestConcurrentInsertDeleteDisjointRangesMinimalRepro), engine/wal, engine/catalog, engine/split, engine/mvcc
gofmt -l engine/btree/insert.go engine/btree/btree_test.go (no output); go vet ./engine/btree/... (clean)

out_of_scope_for_this_subtask: Tree.Delete's symmetric root-changing paths (issue's acceptance criteria and test spec scope this subtask to Insert only)
NodeStore.WriteNode's lack of per-write Sync (pre-existing, separate, unaffected)
Wiring RecordBTreeInsert/RecordBTreeDelete into a real production WAL write+replay path (no such production writer exists today; would be a materially larger, separate change)
